terms.definition: A hierarchical, acyclic, connected collection of nodes with a single root and one parent per non-root node.
A tree where each node has at most two children, conventionally called left and right.
A binary tree whose every node satisfies `left subtree < node < right subtree`, enabling O(log n) search when balanced.
The single node with no parent — the entry point for any tree algorithm.
The number of edges on the longest path from a node down to a leaf; tree height is the root's height.
The number of edges from the root to a node; the root has depth 0.
Recursive visitation orders: pre = root-left-right, in = left-root-right (sorted for a BST), post = left-right-root.
Visit nodes level by level using a queue; equivalent to breadth-first search on a tree.
A BST variant (AVL, red-black, B-tree) that rotates nodes on insert/delete to keep height O(log n).
